data.epi_content:
Hmm, how should I put this.

First of all, I'm on trial.

In the middle of the city, ridiculously wearing a tattered school uniform in front of countless gathered crowds.

It's like an occasional event, or something resembling a festival.

And the people gathered probably think of it as a festival.

The judge uses difficult words while questioning me.

Usually asking if I admit to committing various crimes.

And all I have to do is nod and answer.

It's simple, since everything is already decided.

But rather than just taking me to the square and chopping off my head, the people of this country have a particular obsession, or love, for punishment through procedure.

They even drag pigs that escape from pens and eat children playing in yards to the courtroom for sentencing, so that says it all.

The makeshift courtroom, hastily assembled just for appearances, began to be dismantled immediately after my verdict was announced.

Since it was nothing more than wooden planks fitted together with a few chairs placed around, it came apart quickly.

If you ask what the verdict was, it's obviously death.

An old man in fancy clothes announces my death while holding a gavel, and when he strikes it down, many people cheer.

So loudly that you might mistake the whole world to be resonating.

How ironic that someone closest to death, a withering old man, pronounces death sentences.

It's almost comical, as if he's looking for a companion to accompany him to the afterlife.

"Ha."

Finding that thought amusing, I let out a hollow laugh.

And with that, I became someone deserving of divine punishment, someone the world should despise.

How utterly delightful.

Two soldiers wearing purple uniforms, with strangely shaped swords in their right hands and long pistols that could hardly be called pistols at their waists, pulled my hands behind my back and handcuffed me.

I had no intention of resisting, but these men half-lifted me and began dragging me away.

I tried moving my body a little, but there was no way I could do anything with my frail body.

Perhaps my movements annoyed them, because they gripped my shoulders and arms more firmly, lifted me up so only my legs touched the ground, and simply moved me by force.

It hurts. I'll probably have bruises.

And proving that a noble's disgraceful appearance is a festival for many to enjoy, all sorts of things began flying at me.

Rotten eggs, tomatoes, broccoli, and unidentifiable filthy things.

Especially disgusting was getting hit in the face with mushy rotten vegetables.

As soon as they touched my body, they instantly stuck and began emitting a terrible rotten smell.

"Damn devil woman!"

"She must have mated with a goat! Otherwise she couldn't have committed such a horrible act!"

"She must be a devil worshipper!"

So many voices, voices.

They pass through my head once, making me feel dizzy.

But what crime did these soldiers commit?

They're getting hit with these things just for being next to me.

I wonder if they receive some kind of additional pay for this.

The faces of the people throwing filth at me are blooming with smiles.

For them, this execution is a kind of minor festival in their repetitive daily lives.

But when a noble is dragged out and killed, it's probably better to call it a major festival rather than a minor one.

As I'm being dragged along, I look to the right and see a gallows with four ropes.

And on those long ropes, heads hang like bunches of grapes.

And looking below them, legs stretched straight, perhaps still claiming to be alive, bizarrely move up and down repeatedly.
data.epi_content2:
Swaying back and forth in the air.

In that sense, the idiom "dancing in the air" might be quite appropriate.

At least I won't suffer such a fate.

Being somewhat of a noble, they won't let me die so disgracefully hanging from a rope.

Instead, I'm destined to be cut by a blade.

Even if I had begged the Crown Prince to hang me to death, he wouldn't have agreed.

He would have just disagreed with me and nodded his head as if annoyed.

I can see the execution platform made of wooden planks soaked in human blood.

I step up the creaking wooden stairs.

And as I get closer to the scaffold, the rising bloody scent makes my already empty stomach churn even more.

"Ugh..."

And so, I came to stand in a perfect spot where everyone could look down at me from below, where everyone could look up to witness my death.

Beside me, two executioners in uniform stand next to the shackles embedded in the floor, wearing solemn expressions.

One was an old man, the other a boy.

The old man held an axe that made my knees weak just looking at it. The boy held a heavy sword with trembling hands.

Executioners who only kill nobles inherit the skill of beheading through generations and are treated roughly as lower nobles.

Though in reality, they're treated as butchers.

Like priests or commoner-born magicians.

After all, cutting off someone's head in one stroke or splitting a head in half with one blow would be difficult without proper training.

But my neck could be cut down even by a 15-year-old boy.

It's not sturdy, just extremely thin.

Perhaps that's why the old man brought his grandson to teach him.

The boy, about my height, tries to look solemn while glaring at me.

He was holding a blunt-edged, incredibly heavy-looking square sword with a blade that seemed about 1.5 meters long.

His hands were trembling slightly, suggesting it was quite difficult to hold while maintaining his posture.

In his mind, he's probably repeating thoughts like hostility, or that I am the evil of society.

After all, people are reluctant to kill others without reason.

That's why when fighting in groups, they always create a cause, and when punishing someone, they naturally create condemnation that everyone can sympathize with.

I am obviously a person who deserves such treatment.

The victims are victims of me, an evil, devil-like human.

Whatever I did to those victims.

Regardless, I am obviously the perpetrator.

I was forced to kneel and then shackled.

And simultaneously, tremendous cheers are heard.

In very joyful, merry voices.

"KILL-HER!! KILL-HER!! KILL-HER!! KILL-HER!!"

The scene of them waving their hands to the same beat, shouting for my death, was a masterpiece in itself.

I try to relax my stiffened face, hoping that I can actually die as they demand this time.

My knees were weak with fear, and I felt like I might soil myself at any moment, but I tried to endure it somehow.

However, I couldn't hold back the tears that started to flow.

Once the tears began to pour out, I couldn't control them at all.

So I lift the corners of my mouth and smile.

Because I don't want to show myself sobbing.

When I briefly turn my head to the left to look at the boy, he audibly gulps and then asks me a question in a voice so loud it could burst eardrums.
data.epi_content3:
"Erica-Mecklenburg!! Do-you-have-any-last-words!"

"I still..."

I start to speak but stop.

I don't want to die. It hurts. I don't know what will happen. It's terrible.

The afterlife has always been an unknown realm and an object of fear.

And it's the same for me, so I'm simply afraid.

Perhaps I've been saying I want to die, I want to die, but actually didn't want to die.

More precisely, I probably wanted to live somehow as long as I wasn't too unhappy.

But it's too late now to have such thoughts.

I tried to die many times, and when nothing worked, I tried this.

Unless I'm completely denying everything I've been until now, I shouldn't think like that...

Maybe everyone becomes honest in the face of death, or maybe it's the feeling of having my right to life taken away by others, being killed by someone else's hands, but my emotions keep intensifying.

Whether it's anger, sadness, or irritation, I don't know.

So, as tears continue to flow, before my emotions intensify further, I say the most unlike-me words.

Secretly hoping that the shackles holding my wrists might rust and fall off, I struggle while saying:

"Fuck all of you, you idiots.

I hope everything just falls apart, you, you, you, I just wish you would all di—"

Whoosh.

Splat.

Usually, don't they cut off the head after the speech is finished?

"E..ee...e."

It would have been better if my head had fallen off cleanly.

It hurts so much I could die.

And since my neck is half mangled, no sound comes out.

After some time passed, I felt my body becoming languid.

Or maybe I just couldn't move my body anymore.

Perhaps because all the nerves were severed.

Something like that, anyway.

But how am I still thinking?

Perhaps humans are more resilient than expected, and can live for a very short time even after the head is severed.

For a fleeting moment of a few seconds.

As my head starts to go white, once again with the sound of wind, the blade strikes my neck.

As the world spins and I see the sky, then the crowd reaching out toward my head, I suppose my head has fallen off.

They will now receive the blood gushing from my neck for good luck, and tear at my head.

With smiling faces, feeling no cruelty at all.

Then, I make eye contact with a pretty girl with blonde hair.

She was crying with her fingers stuffed in her mouth.

Please burn my corpse.

Please spit on my grave in contempt.

Please don't cry for me.

And please, let all of this end here.

That was my wish.
